Fastest in FP2 and FP3, then pole by 0.317s; FIA/F1 post-qualifying coverage describes a clean final lap and strong weekend momentum.

F1.com and Toto Wolff report an unexplained, persistent straight-line-speed deficit since Silverstone; Mercedes had not identified the cause after qualifying and Russell called the losses astronomical.
Recent win momentum remains positive and P5 qualifying kept Leclerc near the front, although Spa one-lap pace was below Antonelli and Verstappen.
Qualified P3 with credible front-running pace; the separate explicit grid input already handles his 10-place power-unit penalty, so no duplicate context penalty is applied here.
Ferrari rebuilt the suspension, floor and rear wing after Hamilton's FP3 crash and he recovered to P6; repairs were completed before qualifying, so no continuing mechanical penalty is assumed.
Reached Q3 and qualified P8, sustaining Racing Bulls' positive recent midfield signal.
P7 qualifying was a modest negative relative to the leading group, with no separate unresolved mechanical issue reported.
